title: Rat poison harms wildlife across the state. Major restrictions could be on the way.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Rodenticides pose a serious threat to animals like bald eagles, owls, and foxes.
Lawmakers and advocates say that they are on the verge of a breakthrough on Beacon Hill.
After three owls were killed by rodenticide in Arlington in 2022, residents posted notices warning of the dangers of the poisons.

title: A Mass. restaurant just ranked the best in America for waterfront dining — again
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Restaurants At this Chatham restaurant, diners can sit right along the sand dunes and enjoy beautiful views of the harbor.
Beach House Grill in Chatham.
A beachfront restaurant on Cape Cod is still the ultimate waterfront dining experience in the nation, according to USA Today readers.

title: 17 charged in Randolph street takeover that occurred last fall
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Authorities say participants swarmed police cruisers and set off fireworks during the street takeover.
The first-arriving Randolph police officer reportedly witnessed about 100 people in the street, with some setting off fireworks and others jumping on his cruiser.

title: Modular house falls off truck during I-495 crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Officials say the three-vehicle crash caused delays in Amesbury during the Monday afternoon commute.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A modular home rolled off a truck and onto Interstate 495 in Amesbury Monday afternoon during a three-vehicle crash that caused hours of delays, officials said.

title: Woburn biotech cuts more than 200 jobs after FDA setback
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Business The layoffs follow the agency’s decision not to approve the company’s advanced melanoma treatment.
Replimune is cutting 223 jobs across its headquarters in Woburn and manufacturing facility in Framingham after the FDA failed to approve its latest treatment for advanced melanoma earlier this month.
According to the company, the first round of layoffs was announced on April 10, affecting 63 positions at its commercial organization.

title: Former Cambridge City Councilor Paul Toner gets pretrial probation in brothel case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Toner’s misdemeanor charge will be dismissed upon his completion of nine months of pretrial probation.
Former Cambridge City Councilor Paul Toner.

Matthew J Lee/The Boston Globe By Abby Patkin April 22, 2026 | 1:57 PM 2 minutes to read A former Cambridge city councilor accused of paying for sex at a Greater Boston brothel ring will serve pretrial probation to resolve his criminal case.
